Two quotes required; deviation requires Procurement Manager approval

restricted_suppliers.restriction_reason: Policy restriction for selected device sourcing events
Country restriction in hackathon policy set
Swiss residency-sensitive data scenarios require sovereign or approved providers
Can be used only below EUR 75000 without exception approval
Data sovereignty risk for sensitive/regulated data in listed jurisdictions. Requires Regional Compliance Lead exception approval for these countries.

category_rules.rule_text: At least three compliant supplier options must be compared above EUR/CHF 100000
Mobile workstation requests above 50 units require compatibility review with engineering or CAD lead
Break-fix pool replenishment below EUR/CHF 75000 may use fast-track approval with one quote
Requests marked with data residency constraint must be evaluated only against suppliers supporting residency requirements
Managed platform requests above EUR/CHF 250000 require security architecture review
Reception and lounge projects require business design sign-off before award
Named consultant CVs or equivalent capability profiles are required above 60 consulting days
Cybersecurity advisory suppliers must demonstrate relevant certifications or equivalent references
SEM proposals should include performance baseline or benchmark assumptions
Influencer campaigns require brand-safety review before final award

geography_rules.rule_text: Swiss data residency-sensitive cloud requests prefer sovereign or approved providers
Urgent end-user-device requests in Germany require delivery capability within requested deadline
Business-facing services for France should support French-language delivery where relevant
Large furniture and device rollouts in Spain should evidence installation or deployment support

geography_rules.rule: US data sovereignty: financial and healthcare data must remain in-country; verify supplier US data centre availability for sensitive workloads.
APAC data localisation: India RBI, Singapore MAS, and Japan FISC guidelines apply to financial data. Verify supplier in-country data residency for regulated categories.
UAE PDPL and South Africa POPIA compliance required for personal data processing. Validate supplier compliance documentation before award.
Brazil LGPD and Mexico LFPDPPP apply. Data processing agreements (DPA) must be in place before contract signature.

escalation_rules.trigger: Supplier not registered or sanctioned-screened in delivery country
escalation_rules.action: Escalate to Regional Compliance Lead before award

added_fields.rule_type: machine-readable tag for the kind of enforcement logic (e.g., residency_check, cv_review)
added_fields.trigger_conditions: list of data keys whose values activate the rule (e.g., ['amount', 'country', 'category_l2'])
added_fields.numeric_bounds: object with optional min/max values that must be satisfied for rule to apply
added_fields.required_artifacts: list of document-like evidence that must exist before award (e.g., ['cv', 'certification', 'dpa'])
added_fields.approval_node: single role string that must sign-off when rule fires (overrides generic escalation)
added_fields.exception_approval_node: role that can grant a one-time waiver (used when natural language says 'requires X approval')
added_fields.sovereign_flag: boolean, true when rule is driven by data-sovereignty or residency constraint
added_fields.fast_track_flag: boolean, true when rule explicitly allows reduced quotes / shorter process
added_fields.language_constraint: ISO-639-1 string if delivery language is mandated
added_fields.lead_time_days: integer, maximum allowed delivery lead-time for geography or category

section_guidance.approval_thresholds: Add numeric_bounds (min_amount, max_amount) and approval_node taken from deviation_approval_required_from first entry; set trigger_conditions to ['amount', 'currency']
section_guidance.preferred_suppliers: Add rule_type = 'preferred_list'; set trigger_conditions to ['category_l1', 'category_l2', 'region']; leave approval_node empty (no escalation) unless policy_note contains 'requires approval', then populate exception_approval_node
section_guidance.restricted_suppliers: Add rule_type = 'restriction'; set trigger_conditions to ['category_l1', 'category_l2', 'country']; populate exception_approval_node with first role mentioned in restriction_reason
section_guidance.category_rules: Map existing rule_type; populate required_artifacts by keyword scan (e.g., 'CV' -> 'cv', 'certification' -> 'certification'); set approval_node if text names a role, otherwise leave empty
section_guidance.geography_rules: Add sovereign_flag = true when text mentions 'sovereign', 'residency', 'data localisation'; add language_constraint if text names a language; add lead_time_days if text mentions delivery deadline; set trigger_conditions to ['country'] or ['region']
section_guidance.escalation_rules: Copy trigger string into trigger_conditions; set approval_node from escalate_to or escalation_target; if trigger mentions 'exception' or 'waiver', also populate exception_approval_node

global_rules: Every object must keep its original primary key (threshold_id, rule_id, supplier_id, etc.) unchanged.
added_fields must be omitted entirely when no value can be inferred—do not write nulls or empty strings.
numeric_bounds values must be numbers, not strings; currency codes remain strings.
Lists (trigger_conditions, required_artifacts, managed_by, region_scope, etc.) must contain only unique items.

If natural language mentions both a normal approver and an exception approver, approval_node gets the normal one and exception_approval_node the exception one; never duplicate the same role in both fields.
